description: F.R.I.D.A.Y. workflow skills — sources, categories, ecosystem tags, and install targets.

superpowers.notes: Primary Claude Code skill collection by @obra. Most F.R.I.D.A.Y. workflow skills live here.

friday.notes: F.R.I.D.A.Y. native skills vendored in common/skills/. install-common.sh handles these directly — no clone needed.

builtin.notes: Provided by Claude Code itself or an installed plugin. No clone needed — present when Claude Code or the relevant plugin is installed.

core-workflow.label: Core workflow
core-workflow.description: The skills wired into the CLAUDE.md Workflow Decision Table. Install these first.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Required before any creative or feature work — explores intent and design before implementation.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Multi-step implementation planning — exact file paths, changes, and verification commands.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Executes implementation plans with sequential agent tasks and review checkpoints.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Runs verification commands and confirms output before any success claim.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Triggers a structured code review before merging or declaring work done.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: Guides branch completion — merge, PR, or cleanup decision.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Creates isolated git worktrees before feature work or plan execution.

skills.name: caveman
skills.description: Ultra-compressed output mode — reduces token usage ~75% for scan/parse/boilerplate tasks.

debugging.label: Debugging and testing
debugging.description: Bug diagnosis, test-driven development, and systematic fix loops.

skills.name: diagnose
skills.description: Disciplined diagnosis loop: reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Use before proposing any fix for bugs, failures, or unexpected behavior.

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Red-green-refactor loop for test-driven feature work and bug fixes.

quality.label: Code and content quality
quality.description: Review, skill authoring, humanisation, and README writing.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Creates, modifies, and evals Claude Code skills. Required for Mekiki improve workflow.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Writes and verifies skills before deployment.

skills.name: humanizer
skills.description: Removes AI-writing patterns (em dashes, rule-of-three, filler) from prose.

skills.name: receiving-code-review
skills.description: Handles incoming review feedback with technical rigour, not blind agreement.

skills.name: create-readme
skills.description: Creates comprehensive, well-structured README.md files for projects.

architecture.label: Architecture and design
architecture.description: Codebase improvement, design review, and adversarial planning.

skills.name: improve-codebase-architecture
skills.description: Finds refactoring opportunities informed by domain language and ADRs.

skills.name: grill-me
skills.description: Relentless interviewing to stress-test plans and designs.

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.description: Challenges plans against the project's existing domain model and ADRs.

skills.name: impeccable
skills.description: Frontend interface design, polish, UX review, and visual iteration.

skills.name: prototype
skills.description: Throwaway prototype to validate data models or UI options before committing.

planning.label: Planning and issues
planning.description: PRD creation, issue breakdown, and triage.

skills.name: to-prd
skills.description: Converts current conversation context into a PRD on the issue tracker.

skills.name: to-issues
skills.description: Breaks a plan or spec into independently-grabbable issues using tracer-bullet slices.

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Triages issues through a state machine: incoming → reviewed → ready → in-progress.

utility.label: Utility
utility.description: Output discipline, research, parallel dispatch, and context management.

skills.name: html-preview
skills.description: Decides HTML vs markdown output; generates HTML specs and design reports.

skills.name: find-docs
skills.description: Retrieves up-to-date documentation for libraries, APIs, and frameworks.

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.description: Runs 2+ independent tasks in parallel with zero shared state.

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: Compacts the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent.

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Executes a written implementation plan in a separate session with review checkpoints.

superpowers-opencode.label: Superpowers (OpenCode, opt-in)
superpowers-opencode.description: The @obra superpowers collection for OpenCode. Removed from opencode.jsonc auto-load — opt in here.

skills.name: superpowers
skills.description: Full @obra superpowers collection — install directly into opencode skills dir. See opencode/README.md for opt-in instructions.

notebooklm.label: NotebookLM (custom)
notebooklm.description: User's own customized NotebookLM skill. NOT a bare clone — contains custom auth flow, references/ and data/ layout, local patches. Install by hand using the source below.
customizations: Custom auth flow integrated (see skill header for details)
references/ directory for source PDFs and notes
data/ directory for extracted structured data
Local patches to upstream for F.R.I.D.A.Y. terminology

skills.name: notebooklm
skills.description: Customized NotebookLM skill. Copy from source manually to preserve local patches.

mekiki.label: Mekiki (plugin-native)
mekiki.description: Installed automatically when the Mekiki plugin is active (via /mekiki command). No manual install needed.

skills.name: mekiki
skills.description: Inspect skill-usage analytics — overview, per-skill deep-dive, evidence packs. Ships with the Mekiki plugin.

hanko.label: Hanko (plugin-native)
hanko.description: Installed automatically when the Hanko plugin is active. No manual install needed.

skills.name: github
skills.description: GitHub Workflow Executor — spawns Haiku subagent for git commits, pushes, PR creation, and CI monitoring with human-in-the-loop approval. Ships with the Hanko plugin.

builtin.label: Built-in (Claude Code / plugins)
builtin.description: Present when Claude Code or the relevant plugin is installed. Listed here for reference only.

skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Review current diff at configured effort level.

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Branch security audit.

skills.name: run
skills.description: Launch and observe the project app.

skills.name: verify
skills.description: Confirm a change works in the real app.

skills.name: init
skills.description: Initialise a CLAUDE.md for the current codebase.

skills.name: update-config
skills.description: Configure Claude Code settings and hooks.

skills.name: fewer-permission-prompts
skills.description: Scan transcripts and add allowlist to reduce prompts.
